title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, convicted of multiple counts of rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery for 1989 assaults in Framingham.
Gale evaded capture for over 30 years before DNA technology identified him, leading to his 2024 arrest.
Gale will be sentenced on March 30, following conviction for attacks against two women at gunpoint in 1989.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson was introduced as Providence men's basketball head coach after leading South Florida to a 25-9 record last season.
Hodgson's viral recruiting joke referenced his wife, Jordan Norris, during his introductory press conference with the Friars.
Providence athletic director Steve Napolillo praised Hodgson's recruiting and coaching skills essential for rebuilding the struggling program.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini, 37, from Concord, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for foster care children.
Castrichini draws inspiration from his involvement with Boston CASA, where he serves on the board.
He aims to raise awareness and support for foster kids facing instability and system navigation challenges.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston teachers, parents, and students protested outside City Hall against proposed staffing cuts in Boston Public Schools.
Proposed cuts would eliminate 400 jobs, including 200 teachers and 100 aides, impacting high-needs students.
Boston City Councilors were urged to reallocate $48 million, 1% of the budget, to prevent cuts and support schools.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests are scheduled in Massachusetts on March 28, focusing on democracy and immigration issues.
'No Kings' protests anticipate national participation with over 3,000 events planned across the United States.
Massachusetts protests are second in number only to California, reflecting widespread organized opposition to recent policies.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts support Jaylen Brown as top NBA MVP candidate this season.
Brown averages career highs in points, rebounds, and assists, leading Celtics in Tatum's absence.
Celtics second in Eastern Conference with 47-24 record amid Tatum's Achilles injury and roster changes.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield Police seek help locating 8-year-old Callahan Pierce, last seen near 236 Moraine St. around 7 p.m. Tuesday.
Pierce was wearing a black Marshfield sweatshirt and black sweatpants and was carrying a backpack when last seen.
Police advise residents not to visit the scene to avoid impeding search efforts and ask to check home cameras for clues.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement to start with the Providence Bruins.
Boston GM Don Sweeney stated Hagens' move to the NHL depends on acclimating to the pro game.
Hagens helped Boston College win a Beanpot title, earning 47 points in 34 games last season.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, was sentenced to life for the 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth.
Tracy Gilpin was reported missing on Oct. 2, 1986, and her body was found 20 days later with a skull fracture.
The case remained unsolved for over 30 years until 2018, when Hand's admissions led to his arrest and conviction.

title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was struck and killed while crossing I-90 East in Ludlow on Monday afternoon.
The victim was transported to a local hospital but succumbed to their injuries, according to State Police.
All travel lanes on the I-90 were closed for 30 minutes following the incident for investigation.
